value.body:
## Problem

A ticket can begin in a managed implementation thread with full worktree authority, but a later message sent after the human reopens that chat can be submitted as a generic interactive turn. Historical task records for `UKN-WB555` show follow-up tasks with `metadata: {}` even though the original thread was bound to a repository, ticket, branch, and managed worktree.

This breaks the human review loop: the agent can produce edits and a response, but the ticket workflow cannot reliably resume or finalize the work.

## Desired behavior

An implementation thread is a persistent execution context. Reopening it from a ticket or chat view and sending feedback must resume the same provider session and inherit the same repository, ticket, worktree, branch, authority, and workflow until the implementation is explicitly ended.

## Implementation detail

- Treat `ticket-implementation` as a durable thread property, not a property only of the initial task.
- In `AgentRuntimeService.send`, derive the new task kind and priority lane from the owning thread.
- Copy the thread's canonical implementation metadata into every new task:
  - `repositoryId`
  - `ticketId`
  - `worktreeId`
  - `worktreePath`
  - `branchName`
  - `assignedUserId`
- Preserve `workflowId: ticket-implementation`, `implementation-worktree` authority, provider, model, harness, and resumable provider session/thread identifiers.
- Make the desktop/API "open chat" path resolve the existing ticket implementation thread by ticket/repository instead of creating a new generic chat.
- Add one canonical implementation-context schema/service used by assignment, thread projection, websocket follow-up submission, retry, resume, and reconciliation.
- Before provider execution, reject a ticket implementation turn with a typed `ImplementationContextIncomplete` error if any required binding is absent. Do not allow the provider to run and fail only during post-processing.
- If the referenced worktree is unavailable or no longer resumable, show a recovery action rather than silently downgrading permissions.
- Preserve idempotency when a frontend reconnect resends the same message.

## Acceptance criteria

- Reopening a ticket's implementation chat returns the original implementation thread.
- Every follow-up task remains `ticket-implementation`, not `interactive-turn`.
- Follow-up tasks contain complete repository/ticket/worktree/branch metadata.
- Runtime authority and working directory match the initial implementation task.
- Existing provider sessions are resumed when supported.
- Missing or stale context fails before provider execution with an actionable typed error.
- No path silently creates a read-only conversation for an active implementation ticket.
- Multiple review-feedback rounds can run against the same worktree and branch.

## Tests

- Durable runtime unit test covering initial assignment followed by multiple `send` calls.
- API/websocket integration test proving metadata, task kind, workflow, authority, and cwd survive frontend reconnect.
- Desktop regression test proving "Open chat" attaches to the ticket's implementation thread.
- Negative tests for missing worktree metadata, removed worktree, archived thread, and duplicate websocket delivery.

## Evidence

- Historical `UKN-WB555` follow-ups ran with empty workflow metadata and ended with `Ticket workflow metadata is incomplete`.
- Historical `Work on UKN-CA6U2` was created as a generic `conversation-read` thread despite an implementation-oriented prompt.

## Non-goals

- Changing repository selection for ordinary non-ticket chats.
- Allowing an implementation thread to silently move to another repository or ticket.

frontmatter.title: Preserve ticket implementation context across frontend chat follow-ups

value.title: Preserve ticket implementation context across frontend chat follow-ups
